meta_title: For Founders: SaaS starter kit to Solve no portfolio (Beginner Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS starter kit and overcome no portfolio. A Beginner-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: SaaS starter kit to Solve no portfolio
content.seo_description: Learn how to leverage a SaaS starter kit to overcome the 'no portfolio' hurdle. A beginner-level case study using Antigravity and Claude Code for fast launch.
content.answer_block: The 'no portfolio' trap is a myth; founders don't need a history of projects, they need a live, revenue-generating MVP. By utilizing a high-velocity SaaS starter kit, you can bypass weeks of boilerplate configuration, shipping a professional, production-ready application in hours rather than months to prove your market viability instantly.
content.intro:
You’re staring at a blank screen, paralyzed by the 'no portfolio' ghost. You have the vision for a high-margin SaaS, but the prospect of building authentication, database schemas, and payment integrations from scratch feels like climbing Everest in flip-flops. The market doesn't care about your resume; it cares about the utility of your solution.

The secret isn't more experience—it's better tooling. By leveraging a battle-tested SaaS starter kit, you condense the development cycle of a professional-grade product into a weekend sprint. We are moving from 'I have an idea' to 'I have a live payment URL' in record time.

steps.title: Architecting the Base Structure
steps.description: Initialize your project using a modern stack designed for scale. This ensures your foundation is production-ready from the first commit.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Scaffold a Next.js 15 project with Tailwind CSS, Supabase authentication, and Stripe integration. Ensure strict TypeScript typing and modular folder structure.

steps.title: Implementing Auth & Database
steps.description: Security is non-negotiable. Configure your database schema to support multi-tenancy, which is the backbone of any viable SaaS product.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a Supabase schema for a SaaS application including profiles, subscriptions, and usage_logs tables with RLS policies enabled.

steps.title: Integrating Stripe Payments
steps.description: If you aren't charging, you're just building a hobby. Integrate Stripe Webhooks to manage user subscription tiers.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a Stripe webhook handler for Next.js that updates user subscription status in Supabase upon successful payment events.

steps.title: Building the Core Value Proposition
steps.description: Focus on the one feature that solves the user's pain point. Keep the UI clean and the logic tight.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a functional dashboard component that displays real-time user data fetched from the Supabase API with loading states.

steps.title: Deploying to Production
steps.description: Stop localizing. Push your code to a live environment to begin the feedback loop with actual users.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Configure Vercel deployment settings and environment variables for a production-ready SaaS build.

pro_tips: Use RLS (Row Level Security) in Supabase early to avoid massive security refactors later.
Hardcode the 'Free' tier limits in your DB schema to prevent resource exhaustion during early growth.
Implement a simple feedback modal immediately; your first 10 users define your roadmap, not your assumptions.
Keep your UI components atomic to allow for rapid pivoting of features without breaking the layout.
content.next_steps: Once deployed, focus on distributing your link to relevant communities. Do not add new features until you have your first 5 paying customers to confirm the value proposition.
content.conversion_block: You now have the technical blueprint to bypass the 'no portfolio' trap and launch a professional SaaS. The only thing separating you from the market is execution. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
